001package guiUserLogin;
002
003import applicationMain.ValidationUtil;
004import database.Database;
005import entityClasses.User;
006import javafx.stage.Stage;
007
008/*******
009 * <p> Title: ControllerUserLogin Class. </p>
010 * 
011 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based User Login Page.  This class provides the controller
012 * actions basic on the user's use of the JavaFX GUI widgets defined by the View class.
013 * 
014 * This controller determines if the log in is valid.  If so set up the link to the database, 
015 * determines how many roles this user is authorized to play, and the calls one the of the array of
016 * role home pages if there is only one role.  If there are more than one role, it setup up and
017 * calls the multiple roles dispatch page for the user to determine which role the user wants to
018 * play.
019 * 
020 * The class has been written assuming that the View or the Model are the only class methods that
021 * can invoke these methods.  This is why each has been declared at "protected".  Do not change any
022 * of these methods to public.</p>
023 * 
024 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
025 * 
026 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
027 * 
028 * @version 1.00                2025-08-17 Initial version
029 * @version 1.01                2025-09-16 Update Javadoc documentation *  
030 */
031
032public class ControllerUserLogin {
033        
034        /*-********************************************************************************************
035
036        The User Interface Actions for this page
037        
038        This controller is not a class that gets instantiated.  Rather, it is a collection of protected
039        static methods that can be called by the View (which is a singleton instantiated object) and 
040        the Model is often just a stub, or will be a singleton instantiated object.
041        
042        */
043
044        /**
045         * Default constructor is not used.
046         */
047        public ControllerUserLogin() {
048        }
049
050        // Reference for the in-memory database so this package has access
051        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;
052
053        private static Stage theStage;  
054        
055        /**********
056         * <p> Method: public doLogin() </p>
057         * 
058         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user has clicked on the Login button. This
059         * method checks the username and password to see if they are valid.  If so, it then logs that
060         * user in my determining which role to use.
061         * 
062         * The method reaches batch to the view page and to fetch the information needed rather than
063         * passing that information as parameters.
064         * 
065         */     
066        protected static void doLogin(Stage ts) {
067                theStage = ts;
068                String username = ViewUserLogin.text_Username.getText().trim();
069                String password = ViewUserLogin.text_Password.getText();
070        
071        // Validate username and password format
072        String usernameError = ValidationUtil.validateAsuUserId(username);
073        if (usernameError != null) {
074                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText(usernameError);
075                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
076                return;
077        }
078        
079        String passwordError = ValidationUtil.validatePassword(password);
080        if (passwordError != null) {
081                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText(passwordError);
082                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
083                return;
084        }
085        
086                // Check if user exists in database
087        if (!theDatabase.getUserAccountDetails(username)) {
088                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText("Incorrect username/password. Try again!");
089                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
090                return;
091        }
092                
093                // Check if password matches
094        String actualPassword = theDatabase.getCurrentPassword();
095        if (!password.equals(actualPassword)) {
096                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText("Incorrect username/password. Try again!");
097                ViewUserLogin.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
098                return;
099        }
100                
101                // Create user object with data from database
102        User user = new User(username, password, theDatabase.getCurrentFirstName(), 
103                        theDatabase.getCurrentMiddleName(), theDatabase.getCurrentLastName(), 
104                        theDatabase.getCurrentPreferredFirstName(), theDatabase.getCurrentEmailAddress(), 
105                        theDatabase.getCurrentAdminRole(), 
106                        theDatabase.getCurrentStudentRole(), theDatabase.getCurrentStaffRole());
107        
108        // Check how many roles this user has
109                int numberOfRoles = theDatabase.getNumberOfRoles(user);
110                
111                // If user has one role, go to that role's home page
112                if (numberOfRoles == 1) {
113                        if (user.getAdminRole()) {
114                                theDatabase.loginAdmin(user);
115                                guiAdminHome.ViewAdminHome.displayAdminHome(theStage, user);
116                        } else if (user.getStudentRole()) {
117                                theDatabase.loginStudent(user);
118                                guiStudentHome.ViewStudentHome.displayStudentHome(theStage, user);
119                        } else if (user.getStaffRole()) {
120                                theDatabase.loginStaff(user);
121                                guiStaffHome.ViewStaffHome.displayStaffHome(theStage, user);
122                        }
123                } else if (numberOfRoles > 1) {
124                        guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.displayMultipleRoleDispatch(theStage, user);
125                }
126        }
127
128        /**********
129         * <p> Method: public doInvitationCode() </p>
130         * 
131         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user enters an invitation code and
132         * clicks the button. It validates the code and redirects to the email registration page
133         * with the invited email prefilled.</p>
134         * 
135         */
136        protected static void doInvitationCode(Stage ts) {
137                theStage = ts;
138                String code = ViewUserLogin.text_InvitationCode.getText().trim();
139                if (code.isEmpty()) {
140                        ViewUserLogin.alertInvitationError.setContentText("Invitation code is required.");
141                        ViewUserLogin.alertInvitationError.showAndWait();
142                        return;
143                }
144                String email = theDatabase.getEmailAddressUsingCode(code);
145                if (email == null || email.isEmpty()) {
146                        ViewUserLogin.alertInvitationError.setContentText("Invalid invitation code. Try again.");
147                        ViewUserLogin.alertInvitationError.showAndWait();
148                        return;
149                }
150                String role = theDatabase.getRoleGivenAnInvitationCode(code);
151                guiEmailRegister.ViewEmailRegister.displayEmailRegister(theStage, email, code, role);
152        }
153        
154        /**********
155         * <p> Method: public doCreateNewAccount() </p>
156         * 
157         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user clicks the "Create New Account" button
158         * without an invitation code. It redirects to the email registration page where users can
159         * create accounts without needing an invitation code.</p>
160         * 
161         */
162        protected static void doCreateNewAccount(Stage ts) {
163                theStage = ts;
164                guiEmailRegister.ViewEmailRegister.displayEmailRegister(theStage);
165        }
166
167        
168        /**********
169         * <p> Method: public performQuit() </p>
170         * 
171         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user has clicked on the Quit button.  Doing
172         * this terminates the execution of the application.</p>
173         * 
174         */     
175        protected static void performQuit() {
176                System.exit(0);
177        }       
178
179}
